name: Taejoong "Tijay" Chung
title: Associate Professor
summary: My research primarily centers on understanding and enhancing Internet security protocols for DNS, the Web, Routing, and Emails.
bio:
I am an Associate Professor of Computer Science at Virginia Tech. My research focuses on safeguarding Internet infrastructure through rigorous measurement, specifically targeting the security analysis and deployment of core protocols like DNS, BGP, RPKI, and TLS. I frequently publish these findings in premier networking and security venues, including USENIX Security, IEEE Oakland, ACM CCS, NDSS, and ACM SIGCOMM and IMC.

My contributions to the field have been recognized with several major awards, including the NSF CAREER Award (2024) and the NSF CRII Award (2019), as well as the Outstanding New Assistant Professor Award (2024) from Virginia Tech's College of Engineering. My specific research projects have also earned multiple distinguished paper awards and honors at USENIX Security (2017), ACM IMC (2019), and ACM CCS (2022), along with the IRTF Applied Networking Research Prize (2019 and 2026). Beyond research, my work on RPKI deployment (RoVista) has informed U.S. policy discussions, being cited in the FCC's proposed Internet Routing Security Reporting Requirements (2024) and in NTIA's comments on the same proceeding (2024). I also engage directly with the operational community through invited talks at NANOG 90, 95, and 96, and by serving as a Mentor for MANRS in 2023.

Prior to joining Virginia Tech, I served as an Assistant Professor at RIT and completed a postdoctoral fellowship at Northeastern University. I earned my Ph.D. in Computer Science and Engineering from Seoul National University in 2015.

recruiting: I am always looking for motivated Ph.D. students and postdocs. Please feel free to reach out. For postdoc positions, Virginia Tech Presidential Postdoctoral Fellowships are also a strong route.
highlights: Outstanding New Assistant Professor, Virginia Tech (2024)
ACM CCS Best Paper Honorable Mention (2022)

research_programs.title: DNS and Naming
research_programs.description: Operational security for DNS, DNSSEC, resolver behavior, and the transition path toward post-quantum naming infrastructure.

research_programs.title: Routing Security
research_programs.description: Measurement and mitigation of RPKI and route origin validation deployment, misconfigurations, and real-world security gaps.

research_programs.title: TLS and PKI
research_programs.description: Certificate ecosystems, revocation, delegated credentials, and deployable cryptographic systems for large-scale web security.

research_programs.title: Email Security
research_programs.description: SMTP hardening, MTA-STS, SPF, DMARC, and deployment studies that bridge protocol design and messy operational reality.
